document.docAuthor: Mérida-Rivas, Jorge A., Hinojosa-Díaz, Ismael A., Ayala-Barajas, Ricardo, Pozo, Carmen & Vandame, Rémy
document.masterDocTitle: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Xylocopa nautlana
Cockerell, 1904: 29
. Holotype : California Academy of Sciences , San Francisco , California, USA ( ♀ ). Type locality: Mexico , Veracruz , San Rafael , Rio Nautla.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Description . Female : Integument and pubescence black; wings dark brown with blue-purple iridescence ( Fig. 26 ) with venation dark brown. Body length 26.69 mm (24.67–30.18; n=5); length of forewing 25.48 mm (23.68– 27.38; n=5), head length 7.24 mm (7.09–7.55; n=5), intertegular distance 8.28 mm (7.54–8.83; n=5), width of T2 12.65 mm (11.09–13.36; n=5). Head: With two arc-shaped elevations join in the anterior area of middle ocellus ( Fig. 23 ), such that this ocellus lies between these structures; middle ocellus located above upper margin of eyes ( Fig. 24 ). Clypeus with two depressions toward the middle of the inferior submargin, with large dense punctures on the upper lateral regions; disc and lower margin without punctures; inferolateral regions with sparse punctures; the ocelli almost at the same level as each other, above the frontal elevations.
Mesosoma : Between
the parapsidal line and discal area with sparse punctures, separated by three or more times their diameter. Metasoma : T1 to T3 with very dense minute punctures on discal area as well as sublateral area. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 to T4 with very minute hairs, T5 0.99–1.18. Male : Integument yellowish-orange on head and discal area of scutum. Hyaline wings, venation reddish brown. Body length 32.02 mm (31.71–32.32; n=2); length of forewing 24.84 mm (23.89–25.78; n=2); head length 5.20 mm (5.05–5.35; n=2); intertegular distance 8.29 mm (8.28–8.30; n=2): width of T2 14.19 mm (14.09 – 14.30; n=2). Head : Labrum with a triangular shaped area without punctures on the middle region; rest of labrum with scattered punctures, clypeus with a conglomeration of small punctures on the submarginal area; disc of clypeus without punctures; lateral areas with dense punctures; inferolateral areas with highly scattered punctures larger than those on the inferior submargin, lateral ocelli on the same level as upper margin of the eyes.
Mesosoma : Between
the parapsidal line and discal area with dense appressed punctures ( Fig. 31 ); hind tibia, anterior region with very long dense pubescence, by contrast, above the posterior region pubescence is short and dense; internal surface with long pubescence toward the basal area; internal distal extreme with a robust projection, its width equal to its length without considering the apex, with a notable lobe on the anterior margin ( Fig. 32 ). Metasoma : T1 to T5 yellowish with a black bands occupying the posterior margin; T6 principally yellowish with a slight conspicuous posterior band; T7 yellowish ( Fig. 30 ); pubescence T1 with dense, long, plumose hairs, T2 with a mixture of yellow, long, simple and plumose hairs on anterior margin and lateral areas, discal area with very minute hairs, T3 to T5 with very minute hairs on discal area, lateral areas with yellow hairs, submarginal areas with some dark hairs. Lateral areas of T2 to T7 yellowish; T6 and T7 covered with large dense yellowish hairs, with their apex dark brown ( Fig. 30 ). Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 0.20–0.69, T3 0.31–0.39, T4 with minute hairs, T5 0.26–0.50. Genitalia : Distal angle of the ventral section of the gonostylus, acute ( Fig. 110 ); basal angle at the base of lateral process of the gonostylus, strong, noticeable ( Fig. 109 ); tip of the lateral process ending broad, oval ( Fig. 109 ).

Variation . Some specimens present wings with greenish iridescence, similar to those of
X. mexicanorum
and
X. wilmattae
.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 22–26. Female of
X. ( N. ) nautlana
. 22. Frontal view; 23. Frontal projections; 24. Frontal projections (dorsal view); 25. Lateral habitus; 26. Dorsal habitus.

FIGURES 27–32. Male of
X. ( N. ) nautlana
. 27. Frontal view; 28. Lateral habitus; 29. Dorsal habitus; 30. T5 to T7; 31. Parapsidal line; 32. Posterior tibia, internal region.

.

Commentaries.
Xylocopa nautlana
may be distinguished by its middle ocellus, located just above the two elevation that are morphologically joined in the anterior region of this ocellus ( Fig. 9 ), unlike
X. frontalis
in which arc-shaped projections arise at the sides of the middle ocellus.

Diagnosis. Female and male of majority of species less than 25 mm in length. Female: Frontal area of head without elevated ridges or prominences adjacent to ocelli (as in figs. 34 and 43); Clypeus: lateral areas no protuberant, discal area unpunctate rest of clypeus densely punctate. Area below middle ocellus with a well-defined anterior frontal groove without punctures that initiates with a diameter equal to the width of the ocelli, diminishing to end in a middle elevation between the antennal sockets; ocelli located below the tangent of the eyes; lateral ocelli with pits on the posterolateral region. Malar area equal than or similar to minimum diameter of F1; Mandibles without a notch at the level of the middle tooth ( Fig. 37 ). Area between parapsidal line and discal area of mesoscutum with sparse or scattered punctures. Terga with bigger punctures compared to
frontalis
group (around one fifth the diameter of middle ocellus), pubescence dense. Posterior tibiae: apex of basitibial plate with two asymmetrical dentiform apical processes, anterior dentiform apical process sharp and posterior dentiform apical process rounded. Scutellum angled ( Fig. 38 ). Male: integument in general as in
frontalis
group, with black posterior bands on half the terga; labrum with a small subtriangular area unpunctate on the medial region; rest of the labrum with scattered punctures; clypeus, with scattered punctures on middle region of inferior submargin; lateral ocelli below upper margin of eyes; internal distal extreme of hind tibiae with a prominent subapical projection, without a noticeable anterior rounded lobe ( Fig. 42 ), with blunt margin; internal surface of hind tibiae with abundant pubescence.

paragraph.emphasis:
Keys for species in the
mexicanorum
group as recognized in this study

subSubSection.paragraph:
- Genal area densely punctate; pubescence reddish-brown on head, mesosoma and T1 ( Figs. 43–46 ); integument black, wings yellow with copper iridescence, vertex with scattered punctures; distance between punctures more of four times their diameter, similar in size as those on clypeus ( Colombia , Costa Rica , Panama ).....................................
X. ocellaris

*Males of
X. ocellaris
were not examined
